title: Keleman Ciro
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Ciro}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image=[[File:Keleman_Ciro.jpg]] |name=Keleman Ciro |homeworld=[[Dolomar]]''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' |birth=c. [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]–[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' states that Ciro is in his early thirties as of [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], giving a range of five [[Standard year/Legends|years]] for his birthdate. |death=[[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],The events of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' are set in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''. ''[[FarStar]]'' [[Medical bay/Legends|medical bay]] ''en route'' to the [[Kathol system/Legends|Kathol system]]''[[Endgame]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/Him |height= |mass= |hair=Dark |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings=[[Noell Ciro]] {{C|brother}} |children= |affiliation=*[[Dolomar resistance]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Katarn Commandos]] **''FarStar'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|But me as captain? Adrimetrum's had more experience—at least she ran her own resistance group for a while.''"
"''But you're the one who kept moving in that mind-numbing field Sarne threw at us. Not fast—you were just crawling—but you ''were'' moving. That tells me you've got a better than average resistance to these things; and in a tight situation, that might make the difference.|Keleman Ciro and Lieutenant Judder Page|{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}}} '''Keleman Ciro''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dolomar]]. Along with his brother, [[Noell Ciro|Noell]], he was a member of one of the planet's [[Dolomar resistance|resistance groups]] and assisted in the ousting of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces from his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Following the [[liberation of Dolomar]], Ciro joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|military]] as a [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]], serving with the [[Brak Sector Force|forces]] in [[Brak sector/Legends|Brak sector]]. His service record earned him a transfer to the elite [[Katarn Commandos]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]]. [[8 ABY/Legends|Two years later]], Ciro was a part of the [[Commando/Legends|commando]] [[Squad/Legends|squad]] that infiltrated the planet [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in order to bring down the defensive [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] surrounding the main continent. Despite the successful [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation of the planet]], the ruler of Kal'Shebbol, Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], escaped into the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] with the majority of his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]]. Having learned that the Moff had access to a [[DarkStryder technology|powerful new form of weaponry]], [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Judder Page/Legends|Judder Page]] assigned Ciro to [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] a [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] to chase Sarne down and neutralize him. Although he was a soldier, Ciro was placed in command of the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', a heavily modified [[Starship/Legends|starship]] that had once been part of Sarne's [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. Ciro was [[Battle of Kolatill|captured]] by Imperial forces loyal to Moff Sarne in the [[Kolatill system/Legends|Kolatill system]] early in the mission. Taken to the [[Q'Maere Research Facility]] within the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]], he was physically and mentally [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] and retreated into a catatonic state. [[Standard week/Legends|Weeks]] later, he was rescued by the crew of the ''FarStar'', but eventually [[Death/Legends|passed away]] in the ship's [[Medical bay/Legends|medical bay]] shortly before the ship arrived in the [[Kathol system/Legends|Kathol system]]. ==Biography== ===Early life and joining the New Republic=== Keleman Ciro was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on [[Dolomar]], in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] began in [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]]. growing up in one of the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] underground [[City/Legends|cities]]''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' with his brother, [[Noell Ciro|Noell]]. Ciro planned to pursue a quiet career sitting in front of [[Computer/Legends|computer]] screens as a [[Programmer/Legends|programmer]] and was a student when the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] began to tighten its grip on the planet. Ciro and his brother joined a [[Dolomar resistance|local resistance group]] and participated in a series of [[Guerrilla warfare/Legends|guerrilla attacks]] against [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]]. Shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the resistance succeeded in [[Liberation of Dolomar|removing]] the [[Governor/Legends|Imperial Governor]] from power. [[File:Katarn_Commandos.svg|thumb|left|250px|The unit logo of the Katarn Commandos]] With his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] free of Imperial rule, Ciro joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|military]], along with Noell. While his brother opted for [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] training, Ciro began an intensive training course focusing on [[Commando/Legends|commando]] operations. After his training was complete, Ciro was assigned to the New Republic [[Brak Sector Force|forces]] in the [[Brak sector/Legends|Brak sector]]. Following exceptional [[Ground warfare/Legends|combat performances]] in operations throughout the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], he was transferred to the [[Katarn Commandos]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]].The events of the ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' are set in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''. Ciro was transferred to the [[Katarn Commandos]] two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior. Ciro soon became a valued and respected member of the elite unit, proving his worth time and again. By [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Ciro had attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]] and was part of the New Republic [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|task force]] sent to [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberate]] the planet [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] from the Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]. Rumors had reached the New Republic that Sarne was in possession of a [[DarkStryder technology|potent new weapons technology]], which he had already used to secure victory in several [[Minos Cluster incursions|incursions]] into the neighboring [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]], although his forces were eventually [[Minos Cluster counterattack|repulsed]]. ===The liberation of Kal'Shebbol=== {{Quote|Anyone hurt?''"
"''No, sir. I wish you'd teach Tors how to land these things.|Lieutenant Judder Page and Sergeant Keleman Ciro|}} [[File:BattleOfKalShebbol-TheDarkStryderCampaign.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Katarn Commandos following their crash-landing on Kal'Shebbol]] A small contingent of Katarn Commandos were assigned to infiltrate the planet before the arrival of the [[Task force/Legends|task force]] and disable the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] protecting the main continent and [[Imperial Government Complex|Sarne's headquarters]]. Using a [[Ghtroc Industries/Legends|Ghtroc]] [[720 light freighter/Legends|720 freighter]], the team posed as a simple [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]]. When the New Republic task force arrived in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] and began their assault, the vessel blended in with the other [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] traffic and began fleeing from the attack. However, instead of heading to a designated landing field, the pilot, [[Syla Tors]], began an approach towards Sarne's headquarters. [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Judder Page/Legends|Judder Page]], the [[Squad/Legends|squad]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]], hoped that the mass of freighters heading for safety would disguise their intent. Unfortunately for them, an [[Atgar SpaceDefense Corporation/Legends|Atgar]] [[1.4 FD P-Tower/Legends|1.4 FD P-Tower]] mounted on the Imperial Government Complex detected their approach at the last moment. The [[Laser/Legends|laser]] blast destroyed the freighter's starboard [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]], and the ship crashed, skidding through the undergrowth and slicing through the perimeter [[death fence]]. Ciro and the squad rendezvoused with the unit's [[Pathfinders/Legends|pathfinder]], [[Lilla Dade]], who had been inserted onto the planet on her own three [[Standard day/Legends|days]] previously. She directed them to an underground [[Armor/Legends|armored]] tunnel that had a [[Meter/Legends|meter]]-wide section cut out of it, exposing it to the outside. The tunnel was part of the Imperial Government Complex, allowing the squad to access the base. Reaching the nexus control room at the end of the tunnel, they engaged in a quick [[Lightfight|firefight]] that eliminated five of Sarne's [[Soldier/Legends|troopers]] and gave the squad access to the base's computer systems. Ciro, using his computer skills, accessed the main computer and located the main energy shield control room. Situated halfway across the complex with the main [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] between them and their target, Page expressed his hope that they could find something more convenient. Ciro went back into the computer system and discovered that Sarne had a personal bolthole, closer to their position. Unlike other boltholes, which usually contained a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] or [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], Sarne had stored the [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class light cruiser]] ''[[Ambition]]'' there to use as his personal escape vehicle. Ciro and Page realized that Sarne must also have a cut-off switch to the energy shields hidden in either the bolthole or the [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] itself. Page split his unit in two—half the unit, consisting of himself, Ciro, [[Vandro]] and [[Gottu]], would proceed to the bolthole in an effort to bring down the shields and possibly even capture Sarne. The rest of the unit would head to the [[Prison/Legends|detention cells]] and free any [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] that Sarne was holding. [[File:Ambition_blasts_off.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Ciro and members of the Katarn Commandos attempt to stop the ''Ambition'' lifting off.]] Ciro and rest of the unit hurried to the armored tunnel leading to Sarne's private [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], only to find that Sarne had beat them to it. The Moff had realized that the planet was lost to the New Republic and had decided to evacuate rather than be captured. Commandeering several of his [[Sarne's Stormtroopers|personal guard]], he intended to use the ''Carrack''-class [[Light cruiser/Legends|light cruiser]] to escape the planet, while leaving the base personnel to fight on while he fled. The commandos and [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] exchanged fire, until Sarne used a piece of DarkStryder technology known as a [[mindwarper module]] to incapacitate the commandos. Ciro was overcome by the effects of the module, which acted like a form of [[stun gas]], confusing him and rendering him unable to operate properly. Collapsing to the floor, he became light-headed, detached from his surroundings and forgot exactly what he was doing, although he knew he was doing something important. Despite this, he recovered from the effects quicker that his teammates and began crawling towards the hangar in an attempt to stop Sarne. An accidental discharge from Page's weapon shocked the rest of the squad back to normality, and they regrouped and recovered their senses. Proceeding down the corridor, they entered the hangar just as the ''Carrack''-class light cruiser was taking off. Ciro quickly located a control panel in the hangar and noted that Sarne had set the shield to turn off long enough for the ship to pass through it, then turn back on. Ciro waited for the shield to deactivate, then used a [[Beamdrill/Legends|beamdrill]] to freeze the entire circuit in the off position. Page informed the task force that the shield was down, allowing the New Republic to liberate the planet. ===Command of the ''FarStar''=== {{Quote|With all due respect, sir, neither of us is exactly qualified to command a capital ship.|Keleman Ciro|}} [[File:FarStar.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The CR90 corvette ''FarStar'']] With Kal'Shebbol in New Republic hands, attention turned to the problem of Moff Sarne. Ciro's experiences in the tunnel had been reported, and it seemed that the rumors regarding a new type of exotic technology in Sarne's possession were true. The former Imperial Moff had escaped into the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] with the majority of his [[Moff Sarne's forces|fleet]] and was still considered a threat to the stability of the region. Lieutenant Page was authorized to send a [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] after Sarne to track him down and neutralize the threat he posed. With limited resources, he was forced to use a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] that Moff Sarne had been heavily modifying within a [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] hidden in the [[Sorbiss Valley]]. Renamed the ''[[FarStar]]'', the ship was crewed by a mixture of New Republic personnel and civilians recruited from the planet. To his surprise, Ciro was placed in command of the mission and the ship. He protested at first, claiming that [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], a fellow member of the Katarn Commandos who would act as his [[Executive officer/Legends|first officer]], had more command experience than he did since she had been a [[Siluria III resistance|resistance cell leader]] on [[Siluria III|her homeworld]]. Page countered his argument by reminding him that Ciro had recovered from the effects of Sarne's weapon faster than he had, leading him to believe that Ciro had a greater resistance to it, which would come in handy for the mission. Reluctantly, Ciro accepted command of the mission, but was glad that his brother, who was attached to the task force orbiting the planet, would also be joining the crew. With a commissioned rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]], Ciro was given twenty-eight [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] to ready the ''FarStar'' for launch. Ciro originally planned for the ''FarStar'' to have a shakedown cruise to allow the [[Engine/Legends|engineering]] staff to work out any kinks and bugs within its hastily jury-rigged systems. While still in orbit of Kal'Shebbol, Lieutenant [[Darryn Thyte]], the ship's [[Operations officer/Legends|Operations Officer]], received a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]] from the planet [[Kolatill]]. The planet had been [[Bombing of Domaz|assaulted]] by Imperial forces, and its [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Domaz]], was in flames. Giving the order to belay the shakedown cruise, Ciro ordered the ''FarStar'' to make best speed to Kolatill. Governor [[Monjai]], the New Republic official in charge of Kal'Shebbol, promised to send medical aid as soon as she could.{{DarkStryder|adventure=Omens|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}} [[File:Noell_Ciro_death.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Keleman Ciro's brother, Noell, dies during the Battle of Kolatill.]] Arriving at Kolatill, Ciro immediately ordered relief operations to begin and his [[Officer/Legends|officers]] met with representatives of the [[Kolatill Council]], who informed them that their capital had been attacked by [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]]. Kolatill had rebelled against their Imperial Governor, [[Tetrum]], once they had heard that Kal'Shebbol had fallen to the New Republic. The Council insisted on meeting with Ciro as soon as possible, as they wished to assure the population of Kolatill that they were in control and the situation was in hand. While they were willing to meet with him on the ''FarStar'', they insisted on a meeting on the surface of the planet so that it could be broadcast to Kolatill's other cities as a morale booster. While Ciro was in transit between the planet and the ''FarStar'' in a [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] shuttle, the [[Ton-Falk-class escort carrier|''Ton-Falk''-class escort carrier]] ''[[Kathol Protector]]'', which had been the ship that had launched the TIE bombers that had previously attacked Domaz, dropped out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] for [[Battle of Kolatill|another attack]]. During the [[Space warfare/Legends|engagement]], [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] from the vessel herded the shuttle towards the escort [[Carrier/Legends|carrier]], and it was ensnared in a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] and brought aboard the vessel. The ''Kathol Protector'' then jumped to hyperspace once its [[Bomber/Legends|bomber]] force had been defeated by the ''FarStar''{{'s}} [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[FarStar starfighter squadron|complement]]. During the engagement, Ciro's brother Noell sacrificed his own life to stop a TIE bomber deploying a [[pathogenic bomb]]. ===Imprisonment=== [[File:Captain_Ciro.jpg|thumb|right|140px|Captain Ciro in a catatonic state at the Q'Maere research facility]] Ciro was taken to the [[Q'Maere Research Facility]] within the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]]—a dense accretion of constantly shifting [[Nebula/Legends|nebulaic]] material and volatile [[lightstorm]]s that made navigation difficult—and left in the care of the [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]], [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Langstyn Kraay]]. The facility had originally been a [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] outpost, but had been converted by Moff Sarne to serve as a [[Prison/Legends|penal colony]] and re-education center. At the same [[Time/Legends|time]] as Ciro was dropped off, Sarne stripped the facility of its security troops and weapons and damaged the only hyperspace-equipped ship capable of leaving the Rift. Ciro was stranded on the planet, along with the other prisoners and facility staff that Sarne felt were of no use to him. Interrogated and [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] by Kraay, Ciro's body was battered, bruised, and burnt, and he retreated into a catatonic state. Placed with the main prison population in Dome Four, he was left alone for a few [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] and underwent some treatment for his injuries. However, he retained some automatic functions and could walk if assisted.{{DarkStryder|adventure=Harm's Way|book=The Kathol Rift}} Several weeks later, the ''FarStar'' itself arrived at [[Q'Maere]], and the crew was looking to replenish its [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] supplies since a [[Variant|bacterial agent]] native to the Kathol Rift had rotted its stores. Doctor Kraay, seeing a chance to escape the planet, took the ''FarStar''{{'s}} [[Mission group|landing party]] hostage and held them for ransom. The landing party was placed in with the general prison population, where they encountered a captive New Republic pilot, [[Lowen Chase]]. Chase recognized the ''FarStar'' mission patch on the landing party's [[uniform]]s as being the same as the patch on Ciro's [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]], and led them to him. While they were surprised and pleased to know that Ciro had survived, his mental and physical state shocked them. The ''FarStar'' landing party worked together with the prisoners to engineer a jailbreak and gain control of the facility. Ciro was taken aboard the ''FarStar'' where his wounds were pronounced treatable using intensive [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] therapy. However, his mental condition would take longer to heal and he was confined to the [[Medical bay/Legends|medical bay]]. Ciro stayed in the medical bay on the ''FarStar'' during its adventures through the Rift, remaining in a catatonic state. Shortly before the ''FarStar'' exited hyperspace near the planet [[Kathol (planet)|Kathol]], Ciro finally succumbed to his injuries and [[Death/Legends|died]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I hate when this happens.''"
"''You need to cultivate your sense of adventure.|Sergeant Keleman Ciro and Lieutenant Judder Page|}} Keleman Ciro was a dark haired, non-descript man of medium height. In his early thirties by the time of the ''FarStar'' mission, he was in good physical condition and was in better shape than men a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] younger than him. He was an effective commando and guerrilla, although he lacked the bravado displayed by other New Republic special forces operatives. Ciro was ill at ease at being assigned to lead the ''FarStar'' mission, as he doubted his general lack of command experience and felt that his First Officer, Kaiya Adrimetrum, would have been the better choice as commander. However, Lieutenant Page's confidence in him gave him strength and the conviction that he would succeed in his mission. One of the main reasons Page assigned Ciro to command the ''FarStar'' was that, during Sarne's escape from the planet, he showed that he had a higher degree of resistance to the effects of at least one piece of DarkStryder technology. It was Page's hope that this resistance would give him an edge when facing Sarne. A stern disciplinarian, Ciro believed in the importance of the chain of command and expected his orders to be carried out immediately and without question. He knew that the only way the mission could succeed was by building a focused and dedicated team, a job that was to be made all the harder by the diverse background of the crew. He would also listen to the opinions of others and consider them carefully before issuing his commands. Despite Ciro's misgivings, he was noted for having a genial, gentle style of command by Kaiya Adrimetrum, and she would often wonder what Ciro would have done in her place had he remained in command of the ''FarStar''.{{DarkStryder|adventure=Home (adventure)|book=The Kathol Rift}} ==Skills and abilities== Keleman Ciro was confident of his own abilities, especially his computer skills, which served him well during the liberation of Kal'Shebbol. He was trained in the use of blasters—both personal and those mounted on vehicles—and [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee combat]], and had some degree of climbing skill. Ciro could operate repulsorlift vehicles, and ride domesticated beasts when the need arose. He had some interest in [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]], other cultures and [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]]. Ciro's training as a commando gave him the ability to command others, infiltrate enemy positions, and operate [[Bomb/Legends|explosive devices]]. ==Equipment== Ciro typically carried a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], and wore a [[Blast vest/Legends|blast vest]]. When he needed to, Ciro would arm himself with a blaster rifle during combat situations. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:TheDarkStryderCampaign.jpg|thumb|right|The cover of ''The DarkStryder Campaign''.]] Keleman Ciro was created for [[West End Games]]' ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', published on [[July 17]], [[1995]].{{BN|url=w/star-wars-the-roleplaying-game-box-set/1018175954|text=Star Wars the Roleplaying Game: Darkstryder Campaign Boxed Set}} He first appeared in the short story "[[The Saga Begins]]" by [[Timothy Zahn]]. Although much of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign (series)|The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', including its supplements ''[[The Kathol Outback]]'',''[[The Kathol Outback]]'' ''[[The Kathol Rift]]'',''[[The Kathol Rift]]'' and ''[[Endgame]]'', can be altered to suit the individual playing styles of the gamemaster and players, Ciro's capture at Kolatill, subsequent imprisonment, and eventual death, are required parts of the storyline to allow Kaiya Adrimetrum to assume command. The rest of the adventures within ''The DarkStryder Campaign'' series are written with the premise that Adrimetrum is commander of the ''FarStar''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Keleman Ciro}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} {{1st}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Omens|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Crisis (adventure)|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} {{Mo}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Wildfire (adventure)|book=The Kathol Outback|reprint=yes}} {{Mo}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Harm's Way|book=The Kathol Rift|reprint=yes}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Home (adventure)|book=The Kathol Rift|reprint=yes}} {{Mo}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Rogue Elements|book=The Kathol Rift|reprint=yes}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Nears Its End|book=Endgame|reprint=yes}} {{Mo}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Endgame (adventure)|book=Endgame|reprint=yes}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' *''[[The Kathol Rift]]'' *''[[Endgame]]'' *''[[The DarkStryder Campaign, Deluxe]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=Commander of the ''[[FarStar]]'' |years=[[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] |before=Eventually [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]] (as the ''Renegade'') |before-years= |after=[[Kaiya Adrimetrum]] |after-years=8 ABY }} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |fi=Keleman Ciro }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ciro, Keleman}} [[Category:FarStar crewmembers and personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Katarn Commandos personnel]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:New Republic Defense Fleet captains]] [[Category:Resistance fighters]] [[Category:Technicians]]
